steps.title: Focus on the x-term
steps.content: The question asks for $b$, the coefficient of $x$ in $ax^2+bx+c$. In $$\left(\frac{1}{2}x+\frac{5}{2}\right)\left(\frac{3}{2}x+\frac{3}{2}\right),$$ the $x$ term comes from the outer and inner products.

steps.title: Add the x-term products
steps.content:
$$\frac{1}{2}x\cdot\frac{3}{2}=\frac{3}{4}x$$
$$\frac{5}{2}\cdot\frac{3}{2}x=\frac{15}{4}x$$
Together, $$\frac{3}{4}x+\frac{15}{4}x=\frac{18}{4}x=\frac{9}{2}x.$$ Therefore $b=\frac{9}{2}=4.5$. The $x^2$ and constant terms are not needed because the question asks only for $b$.
